title: LinkLayer peer table O(n) scan on every RX, potential DoS with many peers
description: In rust/lichen-link/src/link_layer.rs:350-367, receive_frame tries every peer's pubkey until one verifies: O(n) Schnorr verify operations per frame. If n=100 peers, every frame costs 100 signature verifications. A flood of frames from unknown senders (or frames that fail MIC before signature check) will DoS the node. This pattern is documented in comments but a contributor adding more peers won't realize the cost. Consider: index by sender IID from frame header before verifying, or document the expected peer count limit prominently.
close_reason: Added documentation to LinkLayer struct about O(n) signature verification cost and recommendation to keep peer count low (<20) or add sender IID hints in upper layers
